blocks.text: But for your holy ones there was very great light.
and were thankful that your holy ones, though previously wronged, were doing them no injury,
Therefore you provided a flaming pillar of fire
For their enemies deserved to be deprived of light and imprisoned in darkness,
When they had resolved to kill the infants of your holy ones,
That night was made known beforehand to our ancestors
The deliverance of the righteous and the destruction of their enemies
For by the same means by which you punished our enemies
For in secret the holy children of good people offered sacrifices
But the discordant cry of their enemies echoed back,
The slave was punished with the same penalty as the master,
and they all together by the one form of death
For though they had disbelieved everything because of their magic arts,
For while gentle silence enveloped all things
your all-powerful word leaped from heaven, from the royal throne,
carrying the sharp sword of your authentic command,
Then at once apparitions in dreadful dreams greatly troubled them,
and one here and another there, hurled down half-dead,
for the dreams that disturbed them forewarned them of this,
The experience of death touched also the righteous,
For a blameless man was quick to act as their champion;
He conquered the wrath not by strength of body,
For when the dead had already fallen on one another in heaps,
For on his long robe the whole world was depicted,
To these the destroyer yielded, these he feared,
